channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Disaster Question Answering with LoRA Efficiency and Accurate End Position
item.description: arXiv:2602.21212v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural disasters such as earthquakes, torrential rainfall, floods, and volcanic eruptions occur with extremely low frequency and affect limited geographic areas. When individuals face disaster situations, they often experience confusion and lack the ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, experiment, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Inference-time Alignment via Sparse Junction Steering
item.description: arXiv:2602.21215v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Token-level steering has emerged as a pivotal approach for inference-time alignment, enabling fine grained control over large language models by modulating their output distributions without parameter updates. While effective, existing methods rely on...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, alignment, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EQ-5D Classification Using Biomedical Entity-Enriched Pre-trained Language Models and Multiple Instance Learning
item.description: arXiv:2602.21216v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The EQ-5D (EuroQol 5-Dimensions) is a standardized instrument for the evaluation of health-related quality of life. In health economics, systematic literature reviews (SLRs) depend on the correct identification of publications that use the EQ-5D, but ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, experiment, attention, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Applied Sociolinguistic AI for Community Development (ASA-CD): A New Scientific Paradigm for Linguistically-Grounded Social Intervention
item.description: arXiv:2602.21217v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper establishes Applied Sociolinguistic AI for Community Development (ASA-CD) as a novel scientific paradigm for addressing community challenges through linguistically grounded, AI-enabled intervention. ASA-CD introduces three key contributions...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, RAG, paper, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EPSVec: Efficient and Private Synthetic Data Generation via Dataset Vectors
item.description: arXiv:2602.21218v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: High-quality data is essential for modern machine learning, yet many valuable corpora are sensitive and cannot be freely shared. Synthetic data offers a practical substitute for downstream development, and large language models (LLMs) have emerged as ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, vector, LLM, prompt, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Reasoning-Based Personalized Generation for Users with Sparse Data
item.description: arXiv:2602.21219v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) personalization holds great promise for tailoring responses by leveraging personal context and history. However, real-world users usually possess sparse interaction histories with limited personal context, such as cold-start...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, augmented, arxiv, LLM, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Field-Theoretic Memory for AI Agents: Continuous Dynamics for Context Preservation
item.description: arXiv:2602.21220v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a memory system for AI agents that treats stored information as continuous fields governed by partial differential equations rather than discrete entries in a database. The approach draws from classical field theory: memories diffuse throug...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, retrieval, memory, arxiv, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Task-Aware LoRA Adapter Composition via Similarity Retrieval in Vector Databases
item.description: arXiv:2602.21222v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Parameter efficient fine tuning methods like LoRA have enabled task specific adaptation of large language models, but efficiently composing multiple specialized adapters for unseen tasks remains challenging. We present a novel framework for dynamic Lo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, arxiv, vector, reasoning, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Measuring Pragmatic Influence in Large Language Model Instructions
item.description: arXiv:2602.21223v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: It is not only what we ask large language models (LLMs) to do that matters, but also how we prompt. Phrases like "This is urgent" or "As your supervisor" can shift model behavior without altering task content. We study this effect as pragmatic framing...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, LLM, study, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Make Every Draft Count: Hidden State based Speculative Decoding
item.description: arXiv:2602.21224v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding has emerged as a pivotal technique to accelerate LLM inference by employing a lightweight draft model to generate candidate tokens that are subsequently verified by the target model in parallel. However, while this paradigm succes...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, arxiv, LLM, model, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Hierarchical Multi-Agent System for Autonomous Discovery in Geoscientific Data Archives
item.description: arXiv:2602.21351v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid accumulation of Earth science data has created a significant scalability challenge; while repositories like PANGAEA host vast collections of datasets, citation metrics indicate that a substantial portion remains underutilized, limiting data ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, GPT, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Refusal: Probing the Limits of Agentic Self-Correction for Semantic Sensitive Information
item.description: arXiv:2602.21496v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While defenses for structured PII are mature, Large Language Models (LLMs) pose a new threat: Semantic Sensitive Information (SemSI), where models infer sensitive identity attributes, generate reputation-harmful content, or hallucinate potentially wro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, LLM, GPT, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ARLArena: A Unified Framework for Stable Agentic Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2602.21534v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic reinforcement learning (ARL) has rapidly gained attention as a promising paradigm for training agents to solve complex, multi-step interactive tasks. Despite encouraging early results, ARL remains highly unstable, often leading to training col...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, study, attention, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Power and Limitations of Aggregation in Compound AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2602.21556v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When designing compound AI systems, a common approach is to query multiple copies of the same model and aggregate the responses to produce a synthesized output. Given the homogeneity of these models, this raises the question of whether aggregation unl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, prompt, model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The ASIR Courage Model: A Phase-Dynamic Framework for Truth Transitions in Human and AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2602.21745v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce the ASIR (Awakened Shared Intelligence Relationship) Courage Model, a phase-dynamic framework that formalizes truth-disclosure as a state transition rather than a personality trait. The mode characterizes the shift from suppression (S0) t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, alignment, model, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Prompt Architecture Determines Reasoning Quality: A Variable Isolation Study on the Car Wash Problem
item.description: arXiv:2602.21814v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models consistently fail the "car wash problem," a viral reasoning benchmark requiring implicit physical constraint inference. We present a variable isolation study (n=20 per condition, 6 conditions, 120 total trials) examining which pr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, retrieval, arxiv, vector, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ProactiveMobile: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Boosting Proactive Intelligence on Mobile Devices
item.description: arXiv:2602.21858v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have made significant progress in mobile agent development, yet their capabilities are predominantly confined to a reactive paradigm, where they merely execute explicit user commands. The emerging paradigm of p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, LLM, GPT, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Latent Context Compilation: Distilling Long Context into Compact Portable Memory
item.description: arXiv:2602.21221v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Efficient long-context LLM deployment is stalled by a dichotomy between amortized compression, which struggles with out-of-distribution generalization, and Test-Time Training, which incurs prohibitive synthetic data costs and requires modifying model ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory, arxiv, LLM, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ACAR: Adaptive Complexity Routing for Multi-Model Ensembles with Auditable Decision Traces
item.description: arXiv:2602.21231v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present ACAR (Adaptive Complexity and Attribution Routing), a measurement framework for studying multi-model orchestration under auditable conditions. ACAR uses self-consistency variance (sigma) computed from N=3 probe samples to route tasks across...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, arxiv, LLM, GPT, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Urban Vibrancy Embedding and Application on Traffic Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2602.21232v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Urban vibrancy reflects the dynamic human activity within urban spaces and is often measured using mobile data that captures floating population trends. This study proposes a novel approach to derive Urban Vibrancy embeddings from real-time floating p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, arxiv, study, model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AngelSlim: A more accessible, comprehensive, and efficient toolkit for large model compression
item.description: arXiv:2602.21233v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This technical report introduces AngelSlim, a comprehensive and versatile toolkit for large model compression developed by the Tencent Hunyuan team. By consolidating cutting-edge algorithms, including quantization, speculative decoding, token pruning,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, tool, arxiv, attention, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Group Orthogonalized Policy Optimization:Group Policy Optimization as Orthogonal Projection in Hilbert Space
item.description: arXiv:2602.21269v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Group Orthogonalized Policy Optimization (GOPO), a new alignment algorithm for large language models derived from the geometry of Hilbert function spaces. Instead of optimizing on the probability simplex and inheriting the exponential curva...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, reasoning, alignment, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SymTorch: A Framework for Symbolic Distillation of Deep Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2602.21307v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Symbolic distillation replaces neural networks, or components thereof, with interpretable, closed-form mathematical expressions. This approach has shown promise in discovering physical laws and mathematical relationships directly from trained deep lea...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> library, arxiv, LLM, model, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Shared Nature, Unique Nurture: PRISM for Pluralistic Reasoning via In-context Structure Modeling
item.description: arXiv:2602.21317v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are converging towards a singular Artificial Hivemind, where shared Nature (pre-training priors) result in a profound collapse of distributional diversity, limiting the distinct perspectives necessary for creative explorat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, LLM, reasoning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, prompt engineering, context, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, chain-of-thought, CoT, audio, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, augmented, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, vector, reasoning, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vector, LLM, prompt, platform, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: fEDM+: A Risk-Based Fuzzy Ethical Decision Making Framework with Principle-Level Explainability and Pluralistic Validation
item.description: arXiv:2602.21746v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In a previous work, we introduced the fuzzy Ethical Decision-Making framework (fEDM), a risk-based ethical reasoning architecture grounded in fuzzy logic. The original model combined a fuzzy Ethical Risk Assessment module (fERA) with ethical decision ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, reasoning, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Uncertainty-Aware Diffusion Model for Multimodal Highway Trajectory Prediction via DDIM Sampling
item.description: arXiv:2602.21319v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate and uncertainty-aware trajectory prediction remains a core challenge for autonomous driving, driven by complex multi-agent interactions, diverse scene contexts and the inherently stochastic nature of future motion. Diffusion-based generative ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, experiment, model, multimodal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, chain-of-thought, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: Distill and Align Decomposition for Enhanced Claim Verification
item.description: arXiv:2602.21857v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Complex claim verification requires decomposing sentences into verifiable subclaims, yet existing methods struggle to align decomposition quality with verification performance. We propose a reinforcement learning (RL) approach that jointly optimizes d...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, reasoning, prompt, alignment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, prompt, example, step-by-step, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Neural network optimization strategies and the topography of the loss landscape
item.description: arXiv:2602.21276v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neural networks are trained by optimizing multi-dimensional sets of fitting parameters on non-convex loss landscapes. Low-loss regions of the landscapes correspond to the parameter sets that perform well on the training data. A key issue in machine le...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, tool, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
